metadata.purpose: Comprehensive data for logistics aviation and China HSR freight comparison
metadata.notes: All specifications from manufacturer data, industry databases, and official sources. Prices in USD unless noted. Ranges and payloads may vary by configuration.

category1_single_engine_turboprop.name: Cessna 208 Caravan
category1_single_engine_turboprop.notes: Workhorse of bush/cargo aviation. FedEx feeder aircraft. Unpressurized.

category1_single_engine_turboprop.name: Cessna 208B Grand Caravan EX
category1_single_engine_turboprop.production_status: In production. 500+ Grand Caravan EX delivered by 2019
category1_single_engine_turboprop.notes: Stretched Caravan. Optional cargo pod adds volume. Most popular variant.

category1_single_engine_turboprop.name: Daher Kodiak 100 Series III
category1_single_engine_turboprop.notes: Excellent STOL. Popular for humanitarian/mission aviation. Acquired by Daher from Quest Aircraft in 2019.

category1_single_engine_turboprop.name: Pilatus PC-6 Turbo Porter
category1_single_engine_turboprop.production_status: Production ended December 2022. 604 built over 63 years
category1_single_engine_turboprop.notes: Legendary STOL performer. Landing distance 130m. No longer available new but significant used market.

category1_single_engine_turboprop.name: PAC P-750 XSTOL
category1_single_engine_turboprop.notes: Extremely Short Take-Off and Landing capability. Multiple configurations: cargo, passenger, skydive, agricultural.

category1_single_engine_turboprop.name: Daher TBM 960
category1_single_engine_turboprop.notes: Fastest single-engine turboprop (330 KTAS). Primarily executive/personal transport. Limited cargo role due to low payload with full fuel (403kg).

category1_single_engine_turboprop.name: Epic E1000 GX
category1_single_engine_turboprop.production_status: In production. ~100 delivered. E1000 AX variant from 2025 ($4.7M-$4.85M)
category1_single_engine_turboprop.notes: Carbon fiber composite airframe. Very fast (333 KTAS). 6 passengers + 1 pilot. Executive focus, not cargo.

category2_twin_engine_turboprop.name: Beechcraft King Air C90GTx
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.production_status: In production (as King Air 260). 2000+ of 90-series built
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.notes: Current production model is King Air 260. Entry-level pressurized twin turboprop. Limited cargo capacity.

category2_twin_engine_turboprop.name: Beechcraft King Air 260
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.production_status: In production. Replaces King Air 250. 900+ of 200-series built
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.notes: Mid-range King Air. Good range and speed. Successor to King Air 200/250 line.

category2_twin_engine_turboprop.name: Beechcraft King Air 360
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.production_status: In production. 360ER at $8.9M. 800+ of 350/360-series built. 7600+ total all King Air variants
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.notes: Top-of-line King Air. Replaces King Air 350i. Longest production run of any civilian turboprop family (since 1964).

category2_twin_engine_turboprop.name: Viking Air DHC-6-400 Twin Otter
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.production_status: In production. 950+ delivered (all variants since 1965). Series 400 since 2010
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.notes: STOL legend. Operates from unpaved strips, floats, skis. 19 passenger seats. Widely used in remote/island logistics.

category2_twin_engine_turboprop.name: Piaggio P.180 Avanti EVO
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.production_status: Production paused/uncertain. ~230 built. Company restructured multiple times
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.notes: Fastest turboprop (402 KTAS). Unique three-surface design with pusher propellers. Executive only, not practical for cargo.

category2_twin_engine_turboprop.name: Let L-410 UVP-E20
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.production_status: In production. 1200+ built (all variants). L-410 NG variant: MTOW 7000kg, payload 2150kg, range 2570km
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.notes: Unpressurized. Rugged design for unprepared strips. Very popular in developing countries and Russian regions. L-410 NG is upgraded version.

category2_twin_engine_turboprop.name: Dornier 228-212 NG
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.production_status: In production (limited). 270+ built (all variants). HAL produces in India under license
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.notes: STOL capable. New glass cockpit in NG variant. Also produced by HAL in India as HAL Do 228. Landing distance 450m.

category2_twin_engine_turboprop.name: CASA C-212-400 Aviocar
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.production_status: Production ended ~2015. 483+ built for 92 operators. Used market only
category2_twin_engine_turboprop.notes: Military/utility transport. Rear cargo ramp. 21-28 passengers or 6000 lbs cargo. Popular military trainer and coastal patrol.

category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.name: ATR 42-600 (Freighter)
category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.production_status: In production. 500+ ATR 42 delivered (all variants). ATR 42-600S STOL variant in development
category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.notes: Smaller ATR variant. Freighter conversion available. 48-seat passenger version. Operating costs 45% less than regional jets.

category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.name: ATR 72-600F (Purpose-built freighter)
category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.production_status: In production. 1700+ ATR 72 delivered (all variants). FedEx is major 72-600F customer
category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.notes: Purpose-built freighter with large 2.95x1.80m cargo door. 7x LD3 containers. Dominant regional freighter worldwide.

category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.name: De Havilland Dash 8-400 (Freighter conversion)
category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.production_status: Production paused/resumed on order basis. 600+ Dash 8-400 built. Cargo conversion programs active
category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.notes: Fastest turboprop airliner (360 KTAS). Package Freighter max payload 10.2t. 8x LD3 containers in F-LCD variant. Range 1555-1640nm with cargo.

category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.name: Saab 340B (Freighter conversion)
category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.production_status: Production ended 1999. 459 built. Active freighter conversion market
category3_regional_turboprop_freighters.notes: Used market only. 35-40 m3 cargo volume. Range 420nm with 9400lb cargo or 1000nm with 7500lb. Popular for regional cargo feeders.

category4_chinese_made.name: Harbin Y-12F
category4_chinese_made.production_status: In production. Y-12 family: 200+ built. Y-12F is latest with wider fuselage, retractable gear, 3x LD3 containers
category4_chinese_made.notes: First Chinese aircraft to receive FAA type certificate (Y-12 in 1995). 19 passengers or cargo. Y-12-II: MTOW 5700kg, payload 1500kg, range 1340km.

category4_chinese_made.name: Harbin Y-12-II / Y-12-IV
category4_chinese_made.production_status: In production. Earlier variant still actively sold to developing nations
category4_chinese_made.notes: Basic utility transport. 17 passengers. Range with max payload only 300km. Widely exported to Africa, Asia, Latin America.

category4_chinese_made.name: Shijiazhuang Y-5B (An-2 derivative)
category4_chinese_made.production_status: Production effectively ended. 1100+ built in China (Y-5 + Y-5B). Used in agriculture and light cargo
category4_chinese_made.notes: Chinese copy of Soviet An-2 biplane. Extremely low cost. STOL from unprepared strips. Piston engine. Still in wide agricultural use.

category4_chinese_made.name: AVIC AG600 Kunlong
category4_chinese_made.operating_cost_usd_hr: N/A - not yet in commercial service
category4_chinese_made.production_status: In development/testing. First flight 2017, water maiden flight 2018, sea maiden 2020. Not yet certified
category4_chinese_made.notes: World's largest amphibious aircraft. Designed for firefighting and maritime rescue. Cargo variant not yet confirmed. Too large for Caravan-class comparison.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: W5000 (Air White Whale)
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: Prototype rolled out Oct 2024. Certification submitted to CAAC. Commercial service planned H2 2026
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: 65 m3 cargo hold. 22.7m wingspan, 22.9m length. Currently the largest cargo drone by payload. Unmanned/autonomous.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: CY-8 Changying-8 (Norinco)
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: First flight completed March 2026. Pre-production testing
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: 17m length, 25m wingspan. Designed for remote, high-altitude, and island logistics. Military/dual-use potential.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: Feihong FH-98
category5_large_cargo_drones.manufacturer: China Academy of Aerospace Electronics Technology + SF Express (China)
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: First public flight 2018. SF Express partnership. Limited production
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: Based on Y-5B/An-2 biplane. Autonomous conversion of existing airframe. Slow but proven platform.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: Sichuan Tengden Cargo UAV (2-ton class)
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: First flight 2024. Rolling off production line in Shenyang. JD Logistics and China Post LOIs
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: General-purpose transport drone. 800m rough runway requirement. TB-001 military variant has 3200kg MTOW, 1500kg payload.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: CM100
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: In development. China Post Group and JD Logistics have signed LOIs
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: 10-ton payload class. One of the largest cargo drones in development globally.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: Dronamics Black Swan
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: EU drone airline license (LUC) secured. Pre-revenue as of 2025. 16m wingspan
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: First large UAV to receive EU drone airline license. Long range but small payload (350kg). Designed for same-day e-commerce delivery.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: Natilus N3.8T (Kona)
category5_large_cargo_drones.operating_cost_usd_hr: Claims 50% lower than conventional air freight
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: Prototype (Kona) flight tested 2023. Blended wing body design. Deliveries targeted 2025-2026
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: Blended wing body increases volume 60%. Carries LD3 containers. Carbon fiber/fiberglass composite. Autonomous.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: Elroy Air Chaparral C1
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: First transition flight achieved. 1500 preorders from FedEx, US military. Production targeted 2026
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: VTOL capability (no runway needed). 8 vertical + 4 forward propellers. 300 lbs payload over 300 miles. Autonomous.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: Reliable Robotics (Cessna 208B Caravan conversion)
category5_large_cargo_drones.engine: Pratt & Whitney PT6A-140 (same as standard Grand Caravan)
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: Part 135 certificate held. FedEx cargo flights active (Albuquerque). $17.4M USAF contract. Uncrewed flight Nov 2023
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: Not a new aircraft - autonomous conversion of existing Cessna 208B. Remote pilot monitors from 50mi away. FedEx feeder operations.

category5_large_cargo_drones.name: Merlin Labs (Cessna 208 Caravan autonomous)
category5_large_cargo_drones.production_status: 25 autonomous test flights in Alaska (FAA $1M contract). Freightways NZ partnership for cargo in New Zealand
category5_large_cargo_drones.notes: Autonomous flight control system retrofit. Safety pilot onboard during testing. Targeting rural/remote cargo delivery.

brand_names.高铁快运: High-Speed Rail Express - general brand operated by China Railway Express (中铁快运)
brand_names.高铁极速达: HSR Ultra-Fast - premium same-day service, SF Express x China Railway Express partnership
brand_names.高铁急送: HSR Urgent Delivery - launched 2023, operated by China Railway Express, 4-hour delivery for select routes
brand_names.高铁快运班列: Dedicated HSR freight trains - full trains with seats removed for cargo, e.g., Chengdu-Kunming line

service_types.passenger_train_cargo: Utilizing spare capacity on passenger EMU trains (动车组). Early morning inspection trains (动检车) and low-occupancy trains used. Packages stored in designated areas.
service_types.dedicated_freight_emu: CRH2A EMU converted to pure cargo configuration. 8 cars, all seats removed. Max speed 250 km/h. Max load 55-70 tons per train.
service_types.hybrid_combi: Mixed passenger-cargo configurations on select routes

高铁快运_preferential.notes: Preferential/economy tier. Prices vary by city pair.

高铁急送_pricing.notes: Launched 2023. Fastest 4-hour delivery. 88 cities, 100 HSR stations.

顺丰高铁极速达.notes: Premium same-day express. Beijing-Shanghai 10 hours. Coverage: 44 cities, 161 routes, 254 trains (as of 2018).

cost_per_kg_example.notes: HSR is ~25% cheaper than air. Truck is cheapest but slowest.

passenger_train_cargo.notes: Limited to small/medium parcels. No oversized goods.

高铁急送_coverage.notes: Covers all provinces except Hong Kong, Macau, Taiwan, Qinghai, Tibet

key_dedicated_freight_routes.annual_volume_2024: 600万件 from Shuangliu (4万 tons), 400万件 to Kunming (3万 tons)

key_dedicated_freight_routes.status: Operational since early 2024. Flagship demonstration line
key_dedicated_freight_routes.performance: 367 days operation, ~100% on-time rate, over 1 million pieces shipped, 7000+ tons total

annual_volume.hsr_freight_dedicated_trains_2024: 200+ dedicated HSR freight trains operated in 2024
annual_volume.notes: HSR freight is still a tiny fraction of total railway freight. Most railway freight is conventional heavy cargo (coal, ore, etc.)

cost_per_ton_km_comparison.notes: Water:rail:road:air ratio approximately 1:2:6:50-80. HSR freight cost is between conventional rail and air.

speed_comparison.notes: For 500-1000km, HSR is competitive with or faster than air (door-to-door) due to city-center station locations and no airport processing delays.

competitive_distance_segments.under_500km: Highway trucking dominates. Cheapest, most flexible.
competitive_distance_segments.500_to_1000km: HSR freight most competitive. Faster door-to-door than air, cheaper than air.
competitive_distance_segments.1000_to_1500km: HSR and air compete. HSR ~25% cheaper, air slightly faster.
competitive_distance_segments.over_1500km: Air freight dominates for time-sensitive goods. HSR too slow.
competitive_distance_segments.notes: This is the key insight: HSR freight's sweet spot is 500-1500km, directly competing with domestic air cargo.

limitations.size_restrictions: Must fit in standard EMU storage areas. No oversized cargo.
limitations.goods_types: Small parcels, business documents, samples, reagents, temperature-controlled pharma, fresh food, precision instruments, valuables
limitations.excluded: Hazardous materials, liquids (most), oversized/overweight items, bulk commodities
limitations.capacity_ceiling: Even dedicated freight EMU only carries 50-70 tons vs 200+ tons for conventional freight train or 100+ tons for cargo aircraft

growth_trends.dedicated_freight_trains_growth: 200+ dedicated HSR freight trains in 2024, up from near-zero in 2022
growth_trends.zhengyu_demo_line: 1 million pieces shipped in first year (2024), daily volume tripled
growth_trends.jd_logistics_partnership: JD Logistics became nationwide last-mile delivery partner for China Railway Express HSR packages (Nov 2024)
growth_trends.sf_express_expansion: SF Express HSR extreme speed service expanded from 44 to 122+ cities
growth_trends.policy_support: National railway logistics cost reduction target: 60 billion RMB savings in 2024 through rail logistics optimization
growth_trends.cr450_impact: Next-gen CR450 trains at 400km/h will further improve HSR freight competitiveness vs air
growth_trends.河南_daily_volume: Henan province HSR freight daily volume grew ~300% in early 2025

hsr_advantages: 25% lower cost than air freight on comparable routes
More environmentally friendly (1:7 energy ratio vs air)
City-center station locations reduce last-mile time
Runs at night using inspection train slots (early morning delivery)
hsr_disadvantages: Limited to China domestic routes (no international)
Limited total capacity per train (50-70 tons vs 100+ tons for freighter aircraft)
Cannot serve locations without HSR stations
competition_with_air_cargo.key_insight: HSR freight is not a replacement for air cargo but a complementary mode that excels in the 500-1500km domestic segment for time-sensitive, small-parcel logistics. It directly threatens domestic air cargo's mid-haul market share.
